SPACEstat: quantitative spatial point pattern and network analysis of the tumor microenvironment

Ziqi, Elias, Aleksandra
19.05.2026

SPACEstat: quantitative spatial point pattern and network analysis of the tumor microenvironment

Ziqi, Elias, Aleksandra
19.05.2026

Agenda

Elias: updated analysis workflow and tutorial notebook
Public datasets for benchmarking
Other types of cancer
Healthy tissue
Project for Rami – define his tasks & goals

Agenda

Elias: updated analysis workflow and tutorial notebook
Public datasets for benchmarking
Other types of cancer
Healthy tissue
Project for Rami – define his tasks & goals

Public datasets

Human lung cancer
Human lung cancer harbors spatially organized stem-immunity hubs associated with response to immunotherapy | Nature Immunology
Report: stem-immunity hub associated with favorable PD-1-blockade outcome. 

smFISH + multiplexed staining
To define hub: windowing approach divided large areas enriched in CXCL10/CXCL11+ cells into multiple immunity hub windows. 

Concern: no cell type annotation!

Speaker notes:
-> Ask the first-author
-> Peter Sorger lab 3D CRC dataset, we can get the data and the annotations, there are observed TLSs and immuno network reports

Public datasets

Human lung cancer
Human lung cancer harbors spatially organized stem-immunity hubs associated with response to immunotherapy | Nature Immunology
Report: stem-immunity hub associated with favorable PD-1-blockade outcome. 

smFISH + multiplexed staining
To define hub: windowing approach divided large areas enriched in CXCL10/CXCL11+ cells into multiple immunity hub windows. 

Concern: no cell type annotation!

Speaker notes:
-> Ask the first-author
-> Peter Sorger lab 3D CRC dataset, we can get the data and the annotations, there are observed TLSs and immuno network reports

Public datasets

Human colorectal cancer (CRC)
The single-cell spatial landscape of stage III colorectal cancers | npj Precision Oncology
Report: 
Developed a novel hierarchical spatial analysis framework - 1) proportions of individual cell types; 2) pairwise interactions between adjacent cells; 3) multicellular local tissue architecture in CNs. 
Among CRC patients with recurrence, their tumors showed enrichment of communities with CD8 + T cells and other type of immune cells.

This dataset is well annotated
Concern: too small window

Public datasets

Human colorectal cancer (CRC)
The single-cell spatial landscape of stage III colorectal cancers | npj Precision Oncology
Report: 
Developed a novel hierarchical spatial analysis framework - 1) proportions of individual cell types; 2) pairwise interactions between adjacent cells; 3) multicellular local tissue architecture in CNs. 
Among CRC patients with recurrence, their tumors showed enrichment of communities with CD8 + T cells and other type of immune cells.

This dataset is well annotated
Concern: too small window

Public datasets

Human Fallopian Tube Precursors to HGSC
Multimodal Spatial Profiling Reveals Immune Suppression and Microenvironment Remodeling in Fallopian Tube Precursors to High-Grade Serous Ovarian Carcinoma | Cancer Discovery 
Report: 
Early lesions are defined by active immune surveillance characterized by the presence of cDC1, NK, and tissue-resident memory (TRM) CD8+ T cells. However, in advanced precursor lesions, we observe a significant decline in these immune cells.. 

This dataset is from Peter Sorger Lab.. 
Concern: Not sure whether annotation is on whole slide or only ROIs.

Speaker notes:
Could be relevant with the single-cell level benchmarking. -> go beyond the proportions.

Public datasets

Human Fallopian Tube Precursors to HGSC
Multimodal Spatial Profiling Reveals Immune Suppression and Microenvironment Remodeling in Fallopian Tube Precursors to High-Grade Serous Ovarian Carcinoma | Cancer Discovery 
Report: 
Early lesions are defined by active immune surveillance characterized by the presence of cDC1, NK, and tissue-resident memory (TRM) CD8+ T cells. However, in advanced precursor lesions, we observe a significant decline in these immune cells.. 

This dataset is from Peter Sorger Lab..

Speaker notes:

Public datasets

Healthy tissue
Organization of the human intestine at single-cell resolution | Nature 
Annotation of spatially resolved single-cell data with STELLAR | Nature Methods 
The intestine dataset was used in Tribus as a benchmark dataset. 
Also have human Tonsil from the STELLAR paper.

Public datasets

Healthy tissue
Organization of the human intestine at single-cell resolution | Nature 
Annotation of spatially resolved single-cell data with STELLAR | Nature Methods 
The intestine dataset was used in Tribus as a benchmark dataset. 
Also have human Tonsil from the STELLAR paper.

Project for Rami

Master’s thesis student, 4-month (June -> Sep)
Plan A

Goal: Demonstrate that SPACEstat uncovers biologically meaningful spatial patterns on independent public datasets. 
Tasks: 
Acquire and standardize 2 public multiplexed imaging datasets (e.g. breast or colorectal cancer cohorts with clinical annotations)
Apply SPACEstat's full metric sets (TSI-proximity, immune infiltration index, hub analysis) to each dataset
Compare SPACEstat spatial metrics vs. global metrics (cell density, abundance) in distinguishing biological groups (e.g. subtypes, survival)

Project for Rami

Master’s thesis student, 4-month (June -> Sep)
Plan A

Goal: Demonstrate that SPACEstat uncovers biologically meaningful spatial patterns on independent public datasets. 
Tasks: 
Acquire and standardize 2 public multiplexed imaging datasets (e.g. breast or colorectal cancer cohorts with clinical annotations)
Apply SPACEstat's full metric sets (TSI-proximity, immune infiltration index, hub analysis) to each dataset
Compare SPACEstat spatial metrics vs. global metrics (cell density, abundance) in distinguishing biological groups (e.g. subtypes, survival)

Project for Rami

Master’s thesis student, 4-month (June -> Sep)
Plan B

Goal: Validate SPACEstat on public datasets and contribute a systematic benchmarking module comparing SPACEstat against existing spatial tools.
Tasks: 
Acquire and standardize 1 or 2 public multiplexed imaging datasets (e.g. breast or colorectal cancer cohorts with clinical annotations)
Design benchmarking framework: same datasets, same biological questions applied to SPACEstat & other methods
Evaluate which tools capture region-aware and interface-dependent features; identify what SPACEstat uniquely surfaces

Speaker notes:
Refactorize the pipeline + feature table creation
Benchmarking
